name: ggez
description: LÖVE-inspired lightweight 2D framework, long-standing and approachable

kind.comment: Code-first game loop inspired by LÖVE (Love2D); no editor or scene model. Pure framework per RESEARCH.md's classification.

license.comment: MIT (SPDX MIT) per GitHub license metadata and README.

documentation.comment: docs.rs API docs are the primary reference; project site is https://ggez.rs/.

first-release.comment: crates.io crate created Oct 3, 2016; GitHub repo created Jan 30, 2016.

latest-stable-version.comment: 0.10.0 released Jun 3, 2026 (max stable version on crates.io as of 2026-07-06).

maintenance-status.comment: Borderline. Repo pushed Jun 22, 2026 and 0.10.0 shipped Jun 3, 2026, but cadence is sporadic — a ~2-year gap sat between 0.9.3 (Jul 2023) and the 0.10.0-rc0 (Aug 2025) / 0.10.0 (Jun 2026) cycle. Alive but slow.

maturity.comment: Long-standing since 2016 with a settled feature scope, but still pre-1.0 (0.10) with breaking changes across 0.x minor versions.

backing.comment: Community-maintained open-source project (ggez org); no company or foundation backing.

implementation.comment: Pure Rust; renders on wgpu, audio via rodio, windowing via winit — no non-Rust engine wrapped.

architecture.comment: Callback-based game loop: you implement the EventHandler trait (update/draw) and redraw each frame from your own state. No ECS or scene graph — the user structures game state however they like.

built-in-ecs.comment: README explicitly lists ECS as a non-feature; bring your own (e.g. hecs).

dimensionality.comment: Primarily a 2D framework. The wgpu backend can draw raw 3D meshes, but there is no first-party 3D tooling (camera, lighting, model pipeline), so scored 2D per RESEARCH.md's dimensionality guideline.

scripting.comment: Gameplay logic is written in Rust; no embedded scripting layer.

data-driven-scenes.comment: No scene/prefab format. There is a conf.toml for engine/window settings, but scenes/entities are built in code, not authored to data files.

reflection-serialization.comment: No engine-provided reflection or game-state serialization; a user can add serde themselves but the framework does not offer it first-party.

hot-reload.comment: No code or asset hot-reloading in dev.

visual-editor.comment: Code-first framework with no graphical editor.

live-inspector.comment: No runtime world/entity inspector, first-party or de-facto.

asset-pipeline.comment: Filesystem abstraction loads resources (images, .ogg/.wav/.flac audio, TTF fonts) by path from a resource folder or zip — better than raw include_bytes! (rating 1) but synchronous, with no processing/reprocessing pipeline or hot reload (rating 3+ would need async loading; 5 a full import pipeline). Scored 2.

cli-project-tooling.comment: No dedicated CLI, cargo plugin, or official starter templates — plain cargo project.

debug-profiling-tools.comment: Ships lightweight timing/FPS-measurement helpers but no profiler integration (no Tracy/egui overlay/diagnostics subsystem).

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling (rust-analyzer just works); no engine-specific IDE integration.

rendering-backend.comment: Hardware-accelerated rendering built on wgpu (since 0.8), which drives Vulkan/Metal/DX/GL underneath. The separate good-web-game sister project renders via a different (miniquad/WebGL) backend for web/mobile.

lighting.comment: No first-party lighting system; custom shaders could implement 2D lighting by hand.

custom-shaders.comment: Users can author custom WGSL shaders and use them with render targets / instanced draws.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: First-party batched sprite drawing (InstanceArray). No built-in tilemap — use an external crate.

particle-system.comment: No built-in particle/VFX system (roll your own or use an external crate).

post-processing.comment: Render targets + custom shaders let you hand-roll effects, but no built-in post-processing (bloom/tonemapping/AA).

text-rendering.comment: Built-in TTF font rendering via glyph_brush.

physics.comment: README lists physics as a non-feature; bring your own (e.g. rapier).

audio.comment: Audio ships in the core crate (loading/playing .ogg/.wav/.flac), implemented on top of the rodio crate.

input.comment: Keyboard and mouse via event callbacks; gamepad via gilrs (behind the `gamepad` feature); touch events are supported through the EventHandler.

animation.comment: README lists animation as a non-feature; no skeletal/sprite-frame/tween system built in.

ui-system.comment: README lists GUI as a non-feature; use an external crate (e.g. egui integration) for in-game UI.

networking.comment: README lists networking as a non-feature; bring your own.

web-wasm.comment: ggez core (wgpu/winit) does not target WASM. Web is provided by the separate good-web-game project, which reimplements a subset of the ggez API on miniquad — scored false for ggez itself per the platform-verification guideline.

ios.comment: Listed as experimental and routed through good-web-game rather than officially supported/tested in ggez core; scored false per the 'only true if officially supported' guideline.

android.comment: Listed as experimental and routed through good-web-game rather than officially supported/tested in ggez core; scored false.

clean-build-time.comment: Not measured on a consistent reference machine this pass; prefer null over a guess per RESEARCH.md. Pulls the wgpu stack, so expected to be moderate, but no reliable figure recorded.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured this pass; prefer null over a guess.

binary-size.comment: Not measured this pass; prefer null over a guess.

web-bundle-size.comment: ggez core is not web-capable; a web build would go through the separate good-web-game project, so N/A for ggez itself.

runtime-overhead.comment: Not measured with a defined methodology; prefer null over a guess.

github-stars.comment: Snapshot from the GitHub API on 2026-07-06.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the ggez crate as of 2026-07-06.

shipped-titles.value: Zemeroth — a 2D turn-based tactical game (released on itch.io); ggez maintains a community Projects list of games built with it.
shipped-titles.comment: Zemeroth migrated to ggez around v0.5 (2019) and shipped for Windows/macOS/Linux/web; it later moved off ggez. Most other ggez users are hobby/jam projects; no large commercial title is widely documented.

community-size.comment: Long-established with a dedicated Discord, but contribution/release cadence has slowed and the active contributor base is small. Borderline small/medium; scored small given the slowed activity.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: A framework rather than a plugin platform — no formal plugin/asset registry; a handful of compatible community crates exist.

learning-resources.comment: Good bundled examples (astroblasto, snake, etc.) plus docs.rs and community tutorials (e.g. the Rust Sokoban book uses ggez), but no official comprehensive book/video ecosystem. Scored 3.
